input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find the exact MAUI workload ID
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target requires the WinUI/UWP build components:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you already have VS, modify it via command line:
# This installs the MAUI and UWP workloads
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK & Java JDK

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK (Android requires JDK 17+)
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Set JAVA_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17", "User")

# Install Android SDK via the MAUI workload's built-in check
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android `
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
```

> **Note:** Run this against a MAUI project (Step 6). It auto-downloads the correct SDK, NDK, and build-tools versions.

## Step 5 — Set Android Environment Variables

```powershell
# Discover what was installed
$sdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Find installed platform versions dynamically
Get-ChildItem "$sdkRoot\platforms" | Select-Object Name
Get-ChildItem "$sdkRoot\build-tools" | Select-Object Name
Get-ChildItem "$sdkRoot\ndk" | Select-Object Name

# Set ANDROID_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $sdkRoot, "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH (for adb)
$currentPath = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$sdkRoot\platform-tools*") {
  [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path",
    "$currentPath;$sdkRoot\platform-tools;$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin", "User")
}
```

## Step 6 — Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Discover available target frameworks in the project
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern 'TargetFrameworks' | ForEach-Object { $_.Line.Trim() }
```

## Step 7 — Android Emulator Setup

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "system-images;android-"

# Install a system image (pick based on above output)
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n MauiTest -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --force

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$sdkRoot\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd MauiTest"

# Deploy to emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 8 — Verify Everything

```powershell
# Full diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
& "$env:JAVA_HOME\bin\java.exe" -version
& "$sdkRoot\platform-tools\adb.exe" devices
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with your actual SDK version (`dotnet --list-sdks` to check).
- The `-t:InstallAndroidDependencies` target (Step 4) is the easiest way to get exactly the right Android SDK versions — it reads what the MAUI workload needs.
- Enable **Developer Mode** in Windows Settings → Privacy & Security → For Developers before deploying to Windows.

Let me provide a comprehensive plan with PowerShell commands.
